our_open_deliverables.text: Investigate Civeo's item-level pricing validation and version-control questions and coordinate a trial or detailed response (including any design-consultant input) for Lananh's scenario
Share detailed documentation on supplier portal security/offboarding processes and ERP integration details with Civeo team

buyer_dependent_items.text: Complete commercial questionnaire (10 questions on licensing user numbers: power users vs business users, Australia vs Canada breakdown) and return to Luke Dougherty/Zycus for pricing estimate
Send post-demo follow-up questions from Civeo team (item-level pricing validation, non-conforming bids, version control, any other Civeo-specific scenarios) to Luke, Shirley, and Amisha

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the only named competitor, mentioned 18 Jun as receiving demo and information from Civeo (8 days ago). This is a light, due-diligence engagement rather than a formal RFP peer; no Coupa discussion on any of the three buyer calls, and Civeo's eval pattern (three Zycus calls in 25 days, buyer-requested follow-up, IT + legal + procurement all engaged, pricing requested) signals Zycus is the primary vendor. Susan Innes (champion, GM Supply Chain) used Zycus at Stanwell (prior role), giving Zycus a trust and reference advantage. Coupa is a medium threat—in the conversation but not yet a finalist—and the window to neutralize is now: deliver the Civeo-specific trial for item-level pricing edge cases (25 Jun follow-up action), run the ROI workshop with Susan + Tyler, and bring in the deal owner's manager for exec alignment within 2–3 weeks. If Zycus moves fast, Coupa fades; if Zycus slows, Coupa could gain ground.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Has mentioned also receiving demo and information from Coupa.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver quantified ROI case (contract-cycle savings, legal-workflow hours, compliance automation) to reinforce Susan's economy-of-scale business case. Position Merlin AI as the differentiator vs Coupa's newer AI. Resolve Lananh's item-level pricing validation and version-control edge cases (committed follow-up from 25 Jun demo) to prevent Coupa exploit. Anchor to Susan Innes's Stanwell success story (she ran Zycus there) and make her the internal reference. Move faster on commercial proposal and ROI workshop to create separation before Coupa's proposal lands.

champion_strength.source: Avoma prep call 1 Jun 2026; demo 9 Jun; demo 25 Jun; Next_Step__c 16 Jun, 18 Jun; Contact role Evaluator
champion_strength.summary: Susan Innes is a strong, active champion with prior Zycus experience (ran it at Stanwell), direct access to IT (Tyler Scott) and legal (Iona Borthwick), and is driving the evaluation and building the business case for global (Australia + Canada) adoption. She requested both demos, asked for pricing, and is completing the commercial questionnaire. Her engagement is current (last touchpoint 25 Jun demo). The only risk is that she does not hold final budget authority and must 'sell it' upward; if the Economic Buyer is not identified and engaged soon, her advocacy could stall at the CFO gate. Champion is not at risk today, but the deal is at risk if we do not help Susan surface the Economic Buyer in the next 30 days.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not identified. Susan Innes (GM Supply Chain) is the champion and is building the business case, but she does not hold final budget authority for a $250k global platform. On the 1 Jun call Susan said 'budgeting wise, having the two of us on the platform helps me get the economy of scale and the return on investment if I can sell it.' The phrase 'if I can sell it' confirms she must present upward for approval. Tyler Scott (IT GM, attended 9 Jun and 25 Jun demos) is Susan's 'interface into IT and Canada because his director manages both countries... IT's global out of US' (1 Jun call), but Tyler is not the budget holder—he is a technical validator and stakeholder coordinator. The IT director in the US (Tyler's manager, unnamed) likely controls IT spend, and final commercial approval for a quarter-million-dollar SaaS platform almost certainly requires CFO or COO sign-off. Neither has been named, contacted, or invited to a call. The 9 Jun demo included legal (Iona Borthwick, General Counsel) and Tyler, but no C-level executive. Without Economic Buyer identification and engagement, this deal cannot progress past proposal to negotiation. The recommended move is for the deal owner's manager to engage Susan to map the approval chain and to request an executive briefing or ROI workshop that puts the business case in front of the CFO or COO, with Tyler and Susan sponsoring it internally.

recommended_moves: Request executive briefing or ROI workshop with the budget holder (CFO or COO) to present quantified business case, with Susan and Tyler sponsoring internally.

Co-build quantified ROI case with Susan (contract-cycle savings, legal-workflow hours, compliance automation benefit) anchored to ~70 contracts/year and legal-workflow pain, and deliver as part of commercial proposal.
Deliver trial or detailed response for Lananh's item-level pricing validation and version-control scenarios within 7 days to close the only exposed fit gap before Coupa's proposal lands.
